commit_scope: Promote POST /api/views/ping into the BML front-door route catalog with native production persistence proof

commands: cd form && ./validate.sh form-stdlib/core.fk form-stdlib/json.fk form-stdlib/cache.fk form-stdlib/form-ontology-loader.fk form-stdlib/source-compiler.fk form-stdlib/kernel-http.fk form-stdlib/language-model.fk form-stdlib/tests/source-language-route-class-template-band.fk
ssh tunnel opened per docs/PRODUCTION-SUBSTRATE.md to local 127.0.0.1:15432 using ~/.coherence-network/secrets/form-kernel-postgres-tunnel.json

curl -sS -i http://127.0.0.1:19086/api/views/ping -H 'Accept: application/json' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'X-Contributor-Id: codex-native-probe' -H 'X-Session-Fingerprint: codex-native-views-ping-20260607' -d '{"asset_id":"codex-native-views-ping-20260607","source_page":"/native-route-probe"}'
local_validation.summary: The BML front-door catalog now includes POST /api/views/ping. The handler parses the JSON ping body, reads typed HTTP request headers, writes asset_view_events, upserts asset_reads_daily, locates the newest content_view source contribution, and inserts an attention contribution when eligible. Go/Rust/TS agree on the lifted kh-header-value-or helper, the Go kernel test suite compiles and serves the BML catalog, and the native route-goal state now counts POST /api/views/ping as kernel-native-high-grammar. A curl against the local Go kernel through the production Postgres SSH tunnel returned 200 OK with X-Form-Router: native-kernel-go, route:views-ping, ok=true, read_daily_written=1, and attention_credited=false for the probe asset.

known_failed_attempts.output: Failed with unbound nil? across Go/Rust/TS because the explicit core prelude was missing from the command. Reran with form-stdlib/core.fk and the band passed with verdict 132226.

Failed with unbound nil? across Go/Rust/TS because the explicit core/cache/source-compiler preludes were missing from the command. Reran with the full prelude list and the band passed with verdict 1049665.
The Go kernel serve CLI treats unknown arguments as source file paths and attempted to read --help. Used the documented serve invocation from docs/PRODUCTION-SUBSTRATE.md instead.

ci_validation.reason: No commit, push, or PR was created in this turn.

deploy_validation.reason: A local Go kernel listener hit production Postgres through the documented SSH tunnel and returned a native 200 for POST /api/views/ping. No public deployment was performed.

phase_gate.reason: Local and production-tunnel proof passed, but CI and public deployment remain pending until the work is committed, pushed, and shipped.

evidence_refs: deploy/front-door/api.bml defines api_views_ping and ViewsPingRoute for POST /api/views/ping.
form/form-stdlib/kernel-http.fk now exposes kh-header-value-or so typed handlers can read request header cells without local list-walk copies.
form/form-kernel-go/server_test.go contains TestViewsPingRouteValidatesNatively, proving the route/handler validation path runs natively without a database connection.
docs/system_audit/native_route_goal_state.json now reports POST /api/views/ping as current_grammar=BML, current_handler=api_views_ping, native_executable=true, high_grammar_native=true.
The production-tunnel curl response included X-Form-Router: native-kernel-go and X-Form-Route-Where: route:views-ping pattern:/api/views/ping request:/api/views/ping.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: POST /api/views/ping can now be served by the native Go kernel from BML against production Postgres for the durable view/read/attention write path.
test_flows: Go/Rust/TS kernel-http band returns 132226 with kh-header-value-or.
Go kernel test suite passes after adding TestViewsPingRouteValidatesNatively.
Native Go listener curl through the production Postgres tunnel returns 200 OK and native route headers for POST /api/views/ping.
Route-goal loop marks POST /api/views/ping as kernel-native-high-grammar and selects GET /api/graph/nodes as the next backend gap.
known_gaps: The Python read_tracking_service also writes an in-process render-events bridge for settlement. That bridge has no durable table and should become a native persisted event cell rather than being copied into BML as process memory.
Header lookup is now reusable, but HTTP header matching remains case-sensitive in kh-header-present? and kh-header-value-or; the next kernel-http lift should canonicalize header names.
